description: Fix npx discoverability for create-plugin and plugin-tools. Running `npx create-opentabs-plugin` or `npx opentabs-plugin` gives a confusing npm 404 error because the binary names don't match the scoped package names on npm. The fix is to add the `npm create` convention command (`npm create @opentabs-dev/plugin`) to the docs as an alternative invocation path, and ensure the plugin-development guide prominently shows all working invocation methods. Known minor issue (not addressed): the `plugin-development.mdx` guide at line 76 shows `readonly displayName` without `override` — this is technically correct since displayName is abstract, but inconsistent with the scaffolder output which uses `override`.

userStories.title: Add npm create convention and clarify npx invocations in plugin-development guide
userStories.description: As a first-time plugin developer, I want to see all working ways to scaffold a plugin so that I don't waste time on a 404 error from guessing the wrong npx command.
acceptanceCriteria: The scaffold section in plugin-development.mdx shows three invocation methods: (1) `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin`, (2) `opentabs plugin create`, (3) `npm create @opentabs-dev/plugin` — with a brief note that `npx create-opentabs-plugin` does NOT work (the package is scoped)
The existing `npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin` example remains as the primary recommended method
The `opentabs plugin create` alternative is kept as-is
The `npm create @opentabs-dev/plugin` option is added as a third alternative following the npm create convention
No other content in the guide changes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/guides/plugin-development.mdx (~lines 12-23)

Currently the scaffold section shows two methods:
```bash
npx @opentabs-dev/create-plugin my-app --domain .example.com --display "My App"
```
and:
```bash
opentabs plugin create my-app --domain .example.com --display "My App"
```

Add a third option using the npm create convention:
```bash
npm create @opentabs-dev/plugin my-app -- --domain .example.com --display "My App"
```

Also add a brief callout/note after the scaffold options explaining that `npx create-opentabs-plugin` does NOT work because the package is scoped under @opentabs-dev. This saves developers the frustration of a confusing 404.

Use whatever callout/admonition component the docs site already uses (check other MDX files for the pattern — likely a Callout component or similar). If no callout component exists, a simple paragraph with emphasis is fine.

Important: The `npm create @opentabs-dev/plugin` command needs `--` before the flags because npm create passes arguments differently. Verify the exact syntax by checking how npm init/create handles scoped packages.
